source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A transverse sinusoidal wave moves along a string in the positive $x$-direction at a speed of 10 cm/s. The wavelength of the wave is 0.5 m and its amplitude is 10 cm. At a particular time $t$, the snap-shot of the wave is shown in figure. The velocity of point P when its displacement is 5 cm is
<image_1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'the snapshot of the wave is shown in the figure'
question asks about point P, whose identity/phase position is only recoverable from the figure
the needed sign/direction of velocity appears to depend on interpreting the plotted wave snapshot
multi_step: requires combining given wave speed, wavelength, and amplitude with the figure-based position of point P
solving needs at least phase/displacement interpretation plus calculation of transverse particle velocity magnitude/sign
multiple-choice options differ by both sign and axis direction, indicating more than a single lookup step
verifiability: answer is a specific closed-form vector expression in short_text format
choices are provided, so the final result can be checked against a finite option set
the physics quantity is well-defined and can be independently recomputed from the stated parameters and figure

agent_assessment.rationale: The text clearly states the wave parameters and asks for the velocity of point P at a specified displacement. The image is necessary to identify the phase/location of P and is semantically clear enough: it shows a sinusoidal snapshot with P on the descending segment above the x-axis. Together, the sample is understandable and the expected answer format is clear.
